headline: No reason for investors to change their tune on software yet, says Brent Thill
summary: Jefferies' Brent Thill says the rotation out of software stocks is still continuing as the sector faces 10 Walls of Worry, and that when investors do return they will be extremely selective. He also says that within the sector, he likes infrastructure software companies and cybersecurity companies, but applications would be the last to come back.

headline: Workday (WDAY) Stock Slides as Market Rises: Facts to Know Before You Trade
summary: In the closing of the recent trading day, Workday (WDAY) stood at $126.6, denoting a -1.98% move from the preceding trading day.

headline: Best Buy CEO reveals successor amid wave of transition announcements
summary: After Tim Cook announced plans to step down from Apple's (AAPL) chief executive role in September, Best Buy (BBY) CEO Corie Barry revealed her successor as she seeks to step down at the end of October. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland dive into the latest round of CEO transitions and departures at major companies.

headline: Software makers' best may not be good enough as AI fears mount
summary: Salesforce will likely report its fastest quarterly revenue growth in three years, but analysts say ‌that may not be enough, as fears that artificial intelligence would decimate ‌software makers have sapped investor confidence in the industry.  Software CEOs such as Salesforce's Marc Benioff have tried to ​reassure shareholders that proprietary data, decades of enterprise experience and in-house AI offerings would keep customers loyal, even as AI tools from the likes of Anthropic encroach on legal, marketing and customer-service work.  • ServiceNow will kick off earnings for ⁠major software-as-a-service firms on Wednesday, followed by Workday and Salesforce likely in May.
